Office machine you might name as a verb when you need an exact duplicate
xylem: Express elevator for sap in a tree
What an elm might call its former partner
yacht: Luxury vessel that lets the affluent avoid rush‑hour traffic on water
Floating mansion where the only traffic jam is a line of other millionaires docked for brunch
yahoo: Boisterous race in Gulliver's Travels that inspired the name of a once-dominant web portal
Raucous exclamation you might shout after finally locating a long-lost email, if you still used the old Yahoo
yanks: U.S. natives who also give a good pull
Rope-pullers who wouldn’t be caught dead sipping tea
yards: Three‑foot measures that keep a football field honest
Back‑yard equivalents when you own several properties
yarns: Tall tales spun on a knitting needle
yawed: Steered a bit off course, as a plane might
Sailed with a slight side‑to‑side wobble
yawls: Two-mast sailing craft whose name sounds like a sleepy exclamation
Small boats that keep their tiny mizzen mast behind the rudder, the underdogs of the fleet
yawns: What a bored audience does when the speaker drones on
Silent applause that often follows a dull lecture
yeahs: Crowd's shout after a perfect guitar riff
Text-message replies that are less formal than "yeses"
yearn: To pine for a pizza after a salad lunch
Feel a deep urge for a Wi‑Fi hotspot you can’t reach
years: What you tally when you add up every birthday since birth
The calendar's plural unit that isn’t months or weeks
yeast: Baker's secret that makes dough rise to the occasion
yells: What a drill sergeant does to a marching band
Sounds you make when the mute button is broken
yelps: Sharp cries a terrier makes when a squirrel darts by
High-pitched pleas a boxer lets out after a too-hard punch
yeses: Positive replies on a multiple‑choice test
Cheerleaders' shouts after a unanimous vote
yetis: Furry mountain dwellers who could double as oversized ski instructors
yield: What a farmer hopes for after sowing corn seeds
Road sign that politely tells other drivers, "Your turn, buddy"
yikes: What you might gasp when a spider drops onto your shoulder
Reaction to the bill after a surprise pizza‑delivery marathon
yobbo: British lout, essentially a "yob" with an extra O
U.K. teenage hooligan, slang for a noisy troublemaker
yodel: Alpine vocal gymnastics that can turn a valley into a concert hall
What a goat might do if it wanted a standing ovation in the Alps
yogic: Pertaining to a stretch that ends with a deep "om".
Like a pretzel after a meditation class.
yogis: Flexible folk who chant "om" while touching their toes
Meditation masters who can out‑stretch a cat
yoked: Joined like oxen under one wooden yoke
So muscular he's practically hitched to his own biceps
yokel: Rural simpleton who thinks Wi‑Fi is a farm animal
Country bumpkin who’d ask if traffic jam is a preserve
yokes: What a farmer fits between two oxen to keep them marching in lockstep
Joint load a married couple might claim to share, figuratively speaking
yolks: What the sunny side of an egg keeps secret
Breakfast's golden secret, often scrambled for fun
yonks: Ages, in the way a Brit says it when it’s been forever
British slang for a period that feels endless
young: Just out of the cradle, still figuring out the word "responsibility"
Like a sapling in a forest—still growing, still green
yours: Not mine—what you claim after signing your name at the bottom of a letter
Possessive pronoun that could follow a courteous closing like “Sincerely,”
youth: Season of life when acne and rebellion seem equally inevitable
What a teen calls himself when bragging about his "fresh" energy
yowls: Midnight cries that could belong to a cat or a wolf
What a cat might do after stepping on a squeaky toy
yucca: Desert plant that might be muttered as 'yuck, a!' when you bite its sharp leaves
Tall, sword-leafed shrub often confused with a cactus in desert movies
yucky: How a toddler describes the green slime in the science lab
Taste test verdict for the mystery meat surprise
yukky: Like a cafeteria mystery meat that makes you say “ew”.
What you’d call a particularly icky situation.
yummy: What a toddler might declare after a bite of macaroni and cheese
Delicious, as shouted by a dessert‑loving dentist
yurts: Round steppe houses that pack up quicker than a picnic blanket.
Portable Mongolian lodgings that turn glamping into a nomadic affair.
zappy: Like a static‑shock surprise that makes your hair stand on end
Describing a cartoon character who runs on electricity instead of caffeine
zebra: Equine that got a barcode tattoo
African animal that would make a horse look underdressed at a formal gala
zeros: Scores that keep the math teacher's grading curve flat
Bank‑account digits that appear after a shopping binge, doing nothing but padding the total
zests: Enthusiastic bits of orange or lemon
zesty: Lemon's personality after a double espresso
Spicy enough to make a pickle blush
zetas: Greek letters that might be used for a superhero squad's name
Last letters of a math series that skips alpha through epsilon
zilch: Nothing on the ledger after the accountant's vacation
What the scoreboard reads when the game never starts
zincs: Coats with zinc to keep rust at bay
Gives a rusty fence a zincy makeover
zines: DIY mags you might spot between the vinyl and the beans
Indie publications that favor glue sticks over glossy presses
zings: Sharp retorts that leave a sting
What you hear when a joke lands with a snap
zingy: Flavor that bites back, like a lemon that just got a pep talk
Lively as a soda that refuses to go flat
zippy: Full of zip, like a hyper‑active hamster on espresso
Describing a speedy cartoon mouse or a commuter who never hits the snooze button
zloty: Polish cash that might make a tourist's tongue twist
Currency that sounds like a sneeze followed by “loty” in Poland
zonal: Like the jet stream that refuses to take a detour, staying strictly east‑west
Regional, the adjective a map‑maker uses when she wants to sound scientific
zoned: What a city planner does to a neighborhood (verb)
zones: Heating-system areas you can set to different temperatures
City sections used to calculate cab fares (and sometimes where you get a speeding ticket)
zooms: What a camera does when it gets a close‑up on the drama
How a cheetah feels after a double espresso
